LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND OFFICER JOHN RUGGEIRO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE DEMURRER TO CAUSES OF ACTION TWO THROUGH EIGHT, THE STATE LAW CLAIMS, IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES DAMAGE IN 2005 FROM ACTS COMMENCING IN 2004. THE TWO YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS HAS RUN ON PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. PLAINTIFF ALSO FAILED TO FILE CLAIMS WITHIN SIX MONTHS OF THE DENIAL OF THE CLAIMS. PLAINTIFF'S STATE LAW CLAIMS FAIL TO IDENTIFY A STATUTORY BASIS FOR THE CLAIMS. THE CONDUCT ALLEGED AS TO OFFICER RUGGERIO IS PRIVILEGED. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION, THE FEDERAL CLAIMS IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. UNDER SECTION 1983, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD WHAT CONSTITUTIONAL RIGHT WAS VIOLATED. AS TO THE A.D.A. CLAIM, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE HOW DEFENDANT DENIED PLAINTIFF ANY BENEFITS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
MINI-MINUTES FOR MAY-06-2008 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-12-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the 1st cause of action which is barred by the statute of limitations. Maldonado v. Harris (2004) 370 F. 3d 945, 954. The demurrer to the 9th cause of action is sustained. Plaintiff has failed to allege facts that support the claim that he was denied benefits as a consequence of his disability. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND OFFICER JOHN RUGGEIRO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: MEGHAN K. HIGGINS, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY (OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3896) APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT. THERE ARE NO OTHER APPEARANCES.
RULING: COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE DEMURRER TO CAUSES OF ACTION TWO THROUGH EIGHT, THE STATE LAW CLAIMS, IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES DAMAGE IN 2005 FROM ACTS COMMENCING IN 2004. THE TWO YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS HAS RUN ON PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. PLAINTIFF ALSO FAILED TO FILE CLAIMS WITHIN SIX MONTHS OF THE DENIAL OF THE CLAIMS. PLAINTIFF'S STATE LAW CLAIMS FAIL TO IDENTIFY A STATUTORY BASIS FOR THE CLAIMS. THE CONDUCT ALLEGED AS TO OFFICER RUGGERIO IS PRIVILEGED. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION, THE FEDERAL CLAIMS IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. UNDER SECTION 1983, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD WHAT CONSTITUTIONAL RIGHT WAS VIOLATED. AS TO THE A.D.A. CLAIM, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE HOW DEFENDANT DENIED PLAINTIFF ANY BENEFITS.
